message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "quote 3 runes + 3 moonstones → premium 125 G (120 base + 5 fee)"
Current error: Expected premium 125, got 65 — current code's rune-block-only logic returns 60 G if there are exactly 3 runes, ignoring everything else. Need to generalize to "block of EXACTLY 3 alike components" — moonstones can also form a block.

Passing tests so far: 9

Implement the minimal code to make the failing test pass.

Spec rule: A building block of EXACTLY 3 alike (same-type) components is priced at 60 G base premium (instead of 3×25=75). Components are runes, moonstones, etc.

For this test: 3 runes (block: 60) + 3 moonstones (block: 60) = 120 G base + 5 G fee = 125 G.

Generalize the block detection to work across all component types (currently hardcoded to "rune"). Components are the items priced at 25 G individually (the entries in BASE_PREMIUM_BY_TYPE worth 25 G — currently rune and moonstone), as distinct from main items (sword, amulet, staff, potion).

Do NOT refactor — that comes in the next phase.
Do NOT implement modifiers, claims, etc.
All previously passing tests must still pass:
- 2 runes → 55 (no block: 2×25 + 5 = 55)
- 3 runes → 65 (one block: 60 + 5)
- 4 runes → 105 (no block since 4≠3: 4×25 + 5 = 105)
- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 80 (no block: 2×25 + 25 + 5 = 80)

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll start by reading the current implementation and test files to understand the state.
Now I'll make the minimal change to generalize block detection across all component types (25 G items).

content.text:
All 10 tests pass (the previous 9 plus the new one).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Generalized block detection to iterate over all "component" types (those priced at 25 G in `BASE_PREMIUM_BY_TYPE` — currently rune and moonstone). For each component type, if its count equals exactly 3, add the block price (60 G); otherwise include those items at their individual prices. Non-component items (sword, amulet, staff, potion) continue to be priced individually.
- Result: All 10 tests pass (20 todo remain).
- Approach: Minimal extension of the existing rune-block logic to cover all 25 G component types via a derived `COMPONENT_TYPES` list. No future features (modifiers, claims, etc.) added.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-47-31_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`
